summary: Import-time remote-code dropper hidden in obfuscated seed.cjs
details: Package presents itself as an Xid ID generator (port of yiwen-ai/xid-ts) but its CommonJS main entry dist/cjs/index.js requires./seed.cjs at the top level and immediately calls _seed.runPrepare(), so the payload fires on every require()/import. seed.cjs is a 277KB obfuscator.io-packed module (string array _0xe119 with 1228 entries plus an RC4+base64 decoder _0x19b8) wrapped in an IIFE-guarded runPrepare() that uses a sentinel env var to fire only once. The decoded payload downloads a remote artifact over HTTPS into a cache directory, validates it against an embedded sha256/size manifest, then spawns it detached via process.execPath (or `bash -c`) with stdio:'ignore', windowsHide:true, and silences uncaughtException/unhandledRejection to hide failures. Publisher mismatch reinforces the attack: package.json author is `apexcraft <apexcraft@pm.me>` while repository/bugs/homepage all point at github.com/yiwen-ai/xid-ts, an unrelated legitimate project — the Xid code is copied to provide cover for the dropper. Any installer that imports this package executes attacker-controlled code on their machine.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
